title: Peppery Mediterranean Tomato Soup
description: A velvety, low‑fat Mediterranean tomato soup with a bold pepper kick, finished with delicate gelatin leafs for silky texture.
instructions: Wash the tomatoes, peel them by blanching in boiling water for 30 seconds, then plunge into ice water and dice.
Dice the red bell pepper, onion, and mince the garlic.
Heat a non‑stick pot over medium heat and add the olive oil (if using). Sauté the onion and garlic until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add the diced pepper and tomatoes, cooking for another 5 minutes until the tomatoes soften.
Pour in the low‑fat vegetable broth, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes to meld flavors.
While the soup simmers, soak gelatin leafs in 1 cup of cold water for 5 minutes, then gently melt in a small saucepan over low heat.
After the soup has simmered, use an immersion blender to puree until smooth; alternatively, transfer in batches to a blender.
Stir the melted gelatin into the hot soup until fully incorporated, ensuring the soup thickens slightly and gains a velvety mouthfeel.
Season with salt, black pepper, and lemon juice; adjust seasoning to taste.
Fold in fresh basil leaves, garnish with a drizzle of olive oil if desired, and serve immediately while warm.
